description: Cosa Nostra is a Sicilian mafia-type criminal organization based in Italy, primarily in Sicily, with international branches. It originated in the 19th century and is known for its hierarchical structure and involvement in various illegal activities. The Italian state intensified countermeasures in the 1980s, leading to high-profile assassinations of anti-mafia magistrates.

country_links.evidence_quote: la droga passante dal Belgio e dai Paesi Bassi in Piemonte e Lombardia in alleanza con le cosche del trapanese
country_links.context: Cosa Nostra routed drugs through Belgium to Piedmont and Lombardy.

country_links.evidence_quote: una volta catturato in Brasile dove era fuggito
country_links.context: Tommaso Buscetta, a Cosa Nostra member, was captured in Brazil after fleeing there.

country_links.evidence_quote: La droga proveniva dalla Colombia e passava dalla Namibia.
country_links.context: Cosa Nostra sourced cocaine from Colombia, which was routed through Namibia.

country_links.evidence_quote: la droga colombiana spedita attraverso i porti di Anversa e Amburgo.
country_links.context: Cosa Nostra received Colombian cocaine shipped through the port of Hamburg.

country_links.evidence_quote: il traffico dell'eroina fruttava a Cosa nostra un ricavato di circa 300 milioni di dollari l'anno soltanto negli Stati Uniti ed infatti numerosi siciliani vi si trasferirono per controllare direttamente questo 'business'
country_links.context: Cosa Nostra trafficked heroin into the United States and had members relocate there to control the business.

country_links.evidence_quote: le famiglie dei Belfiore, Ursino - Macrì, queste contrattavano la droga con i siciliani dei Cuntrera-Caruana residenti in Venezuela.
country_links.context: Cosa Nostra families Cuntrera-Caruana were based in Venezuela, supplying drugs to 'ndrine in Turin.
